text: Agile methodology is a flexible approach to software development. Instead of planning the entire project upfront, teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually two weeks long. Each sprint focuses on a set of tasks from the backlog, which is a prioritised list of work. Team members describe features through user stories, simple statements of what a user needs. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to share progress, discuss challenges, and plan the day. This iterative development allows teams to adapt quickly, delivering improvements step by step based on feedback. Agile helps teams stay efficient and responsive to change.
